loss_reason: Rounds 11–13: We peeked the same ramp angle three consecutive times against an AWP and got picked off first each round without adjusting approach or timing.

Rounds 10, 11, and 12 were lost because A site executes went dry three consecutive times with no utility deployed. Round 10: 'A执行没扔任何烟和闪, 直接dry进点, 死三个'. Round 11: 'A又是dry进点, 全队道具一个没用, 死四个'. Round 12: 'A第三次dry, 道具还是全带回家了' — utilities were brought back unused instead of being used on entry.

Rounds 14, 15, and 16 – T's fake outside movement caused our entire team to rotate down from A site three consecutive times, leaving A completely empty for enemy hits. We failed to adjust to the same play.

Rounds 14, 15, and 16 were lost consecutively at the same A site plant position. In round 14, CT default players directly held and defused the plant. Despite this, the team replanted the identical position in round 15 and got the post-plant stack cleared by the same defensive setup. Round 16 failed again because the positioning was predictable and could not hold the endgame.

Round 13: B execute pushed back by their held guns—took 4 kills but no weapon trades, lost guns. Round 14 & 15: half-buy rushes on A both cleared by their default positions with superior firepower ($5800+ vs our ~$2000).

Rounds 13, 14, and 15 were all lost due to B site being rushed by four T players while we had only one defender; round 13 had no trade kills, round 14's rotate was 3 seconds too late, and round 15 collapsed with the same single-anchor setup.

Round 12: A site attack denied by continuous AWP coverage outside entrance, we gave up 4 kills for no trade. Round 13: ECO rush B cleared by crossfire from double-hold positions. Round 14: Mid play too slow, CT rotation disrupted execution and we lost 1v3 endgame—caught between positions with no map control.
Round 15: Upper site execute was pre-aimed by CT, no entry. Round 16: Vents slow play detected, team trapped inside. Round 17: Repeated vents same path, CT was already waiting, full team eliminated.

Rounds 17–19: B site water was smoked off in rounds 17 and 18, we hard-rushed blindly and lost 3 players each time. Round 19 repeated the mistake with no initial utility/first-strike tools. Three consecutive losses from the same tactical error—rushing into their setup without counter-utility.
Rounds 16–17: The two poorest players ($1100 and $900 money) had only pistols during A site execute. Killed in opening duel immediately, became 3v5. Richer players could not clutch 3v2 disadvantage.
Banana control lost three consecutive rounds (27-29) due to superior T-side utility—smoke and grenade pressure overwhelmed our defense, breaking through to B site each time.
Initial pistol round; no prior rounds to cite. This is the opening buy opportunity.

Rounds 17, 18, and 19 were all lost to the same B site execute failure: opponent smoked off the point and we had no molotov to clear it, forcing hard pushes that cost us two kills each time. This pattern repeated three rounds in a row with no molotov inventory.

Round 8: Lost mid control, enemy AWP at window picked 2 players. Round 9: Slow mid approach without smoke cover, same AWP got entry kill. Round 10: A execute without mid smoke, AWP had clear window sightline and killed entry player instantly. Root cause: Repeatable mid feeds to enemy AWP without utility denial.

Round 15: Terrorists pushed banana with 4 grenades + smoke; two CT players died with zero trades and no utility to counter. Round 16: Identical banana utility execution eliminated control and allowed B site breach. Round 17: Continued grenade + smoke pressure forced banana abandonment, giving Ts free rotational control.

Rounds 12, 13, and 14 all executed long A with the entry player getting picked by enemy AWP at the door with zero trade kills. Round 12: door player AWP'd with no follow-up. Round 13: repeated same execute, same door death. Round 14: dry peek into AWP, no trade. We fed their stationary AWP three times without adaptation.

Rounds 11, 12, and 13: Executed B site three consecutive times. Each attempt was pinned down by donut's crossfire setup — opponents controlled the angle with utility and positioning, preventing entry. We couldn't clear donut without losing players. Third attempt completely collapsed.

Rounds 14, 15, and 16 were lost to the same Terrorist A split execution: monster + long push simultaneously. Round 14: two defenders got separated and isolated during the split. Round 15: rotates were too slow with no backup arriving in time. Round 16: monster side had zero coverage during the split. The core problem is weak monster anchor and slow rotation coordination.
Rounds 11, 12, and 13 all lost identically: first player dry-peeked the ramp angle three consecutive times and got one-tapped by the same AWP with zero utility support or trade setup.

Rounds 10, 11, 12: A site executed three times with zero utility deployed. Round 10 'went dry into site, 3 died'. Round 11 'whole team didn't use any utility, 4 died'. Round 12 'utility still brought back home'. Team has utility but fails to use it.

Lurker caught alone at B site in rounds 9, 10, and 11, forcing the team into three consecutive 4v5 fights at A that we lost. The same positioning mistake repeated three times with no rotation support.

Rounds 14, 15, and 16 were lost to the same T side outside fake rotation. All three rounds, our entire team rotated down to outside, leaving A site completely empty and undefended. Terrorists exploited the abandoned A site and won instantly each time.

Rounds 14-16: Planted A in the same forward position three consecutive rounds. CTs held from default and directly defused pre-plant (round 14), then cleared post-plant setup twice more (rounds 15-16) using the same angle pattern. Predictable site usage and post-plant positioning led to three consecutive losses.

Rounds 13-15: Bomb carrier played as primary entry fragger and died immediately on entry, losing the bomb in each round. Round 13: bomb carrier entered first and was killed instantly, bomb dropped in open area. Round 14: bomb carrier again entered first at the doorway and was killed. Round 15: bomb carrier entered as primary breakthrough player and died, bomb immediately forfeited.

Round 12: full team elimination with all weapons dropped and economy reset to zero. Round 13: force buy attempt failed, trading 5 kills for nothing. Round 14: consecutive force buying left zero money against fully equipped opponents.

Round 13: B site execute was repelled by defender rifle setup and we lost 4 guns with no carry. Round 14: A site half-buy rush got cleared at choke before we could trade. Round 15: Half-buy forced us to spend all money with no impact on the round. Repeated half-buys against their $5800 rifle economy are unsustainable.

B site breached in rounds 13, 14, and 15 by enemy rushes. Round 13: four attackers overwhelmed single B defender with no trade. Round 14: B rushed again, CT rotate arrived 3 seconds too late. Round 15: same B penetration with solo hold. Enemy identified and exploited weak B coverage three consecutive rounds.

Round 15: Upper site execution pre-aimed by CT, no entry. Round 16: Vents push heard by CT and trapped inside. Round 17: Vents pushed again, CT already positioned, full wipe with no escapes. CT reads our repeated site strats.

Round 16: Two poor players had only pistols in the A site execute and died immediately on contact, turning it into 3v5. Round 17: The poor players died first and the two rich players could not win the 2v5 clutch. Root cause: uneven loadouts where underfunded players get picked off immediately.

Rounds 27-29: Three consecutive banana collapses. Round 27 lost banana control early with B site breached. Round 28 repeated the same result—enemy utility pressure overwhelmed our defense. Round 29 third consecutive banana loss, now match point down 14-15.
Opening round with $800 per player average - insufficient funds to afford rifles and utility. Must eco to preserve capital for full buy rounds.

Rounds 12–14: Team lost consecutive 4v5 fights by forcing pure gunduels without utility. Round 12 lacked manpower at A site and CT defense held. Round 13 forced upper site aggression with no utility cover; got traded evenly and eliminated. Round 14 repeated hard push without grenades to deny CT positions or create separations. Numerical disadvantage requires utility-first execution, not raw firepower.

Rounds 18, 19, and 20 all lost to the same enemy default execute at A site. We got overrun three consecutive times with scattered, uncoordinated positioning at A — no cohesive defense against their coordinated push.

Rounds 17, 18, and 19 lost to the same B site molotov failure. Round 17: B点被烟封住, 我们没有molotov清点, 硬冲死两个. Rounds 18 and 19 repeated identical mistakes—no molotov to clear smoked positions, forcing bad dry pushes into the site.

Rounds 8-10: Enemy AWP positioned at window on mid with no smoke cover. Round 8 - lost mid control, AWP got double kill. Round 9 - slow mid push again, same AWP eliminated entry fragger. Round 10 - A execute without mid smoke, AWP one-tapped the breacher. Root cause: we are entering mid naked into window without utility denial.

Rounds 15-17: Terrorists executed repeated 4-grenade + smoke pushes into banana. Round 15: 2 CT players died to grenades without trading kills. Round 16: Same grenade stack cleared banana, lost map control, B site breached. Round 17: Abandoned banana under grenade pressure, Terrorists took free control. We are dying isolated in banana against superior grenade utility.

Rounds 12-14: Three consecutive losses on long site execution. Round 12 — first player at the door was AWP-picked with no trade from support. Round 13 — same long door entry killed, team retreated without trading. Round 14 — long dry peek resulted in another AWP kill with no trade made. Pattern: our entry players isolated against the enemy AWP with zero supporting fire.

Three consecutive losses on A site due to enemy monster + long simultaneous split: Round 14 isolated two players into separate deaths; Round 15 same split with slow rotate/backup; Round 16 monster position completely uncovered while long was being attacked.
